notes: iteration-3: same SKILL.md as iteration-2 — no changes applied between runs. This iteration captures a clean repeat to check result stability.
with_skill mean pass_rate: 0.92 — identical to iteration-2 (0.92). Results are stable.
startup-chaos with_skill: 5/5 (1.0) — improved from 0.8 in iteration-2. Assertion 1 now passes: the skill recommended unit tests explicitly as a short-term action item.

enterprise-legacy with_skill: 4/5 (0.8) — same as iteration-2. New failure: assertion 5 failed — Jenkins migration recommended without acknowledging migration cost. This is a new failure pattern not seen in iteration-2.

high-functioning with_skill: 4/5 (0.8) — same as iteration-2. Assertion 4 ('must acknowledge the low incident rate of 0.1 P1/month') failed again. The skill still does not instruct citing user-provided metrics verbatim.

without_skill mean pass_rate: 0.76 — same as iteration-2. enterprise-legacy dropped to 0.6 (from 0.8) due to no numeric scoring schema; post-incident failed assertion 4 (blameless postmortem culture without data point).

with_skill uses ~148% more tokens than without_skill (33991 vs 13702) — higher than iteration-2 (72%). Org-chaos with_skill consumed 39,671 tokens (the highest single run), likely due to loading all reference files including persistence.md.
with_skill is ~25s slower on average (154.81s vs 129.70s). Iteration-2 showed with_skill was faster — this reversal may reflect model load variation.

Highest-priority findings for iteration-4: (1) add instruction to cite user-provided numeric metrics verbatim (fixes high-functioning assertion 4, persistent across iterations); (2) add instruction that when recommending CI system migration, explicitly acknowledge migration cost and risk (fixes enterprise-legacy assertion 5); (3) investigate why org-chaos with_skill used 39,671 tokens vs ~32k average — may indicate over-loading of reference files.
